"<b>Diastereomers&nbsp;</b>differ in <i>configuration </i>at <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;some&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;&#x28;&lt;u&gt;not&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;all&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> chiral centers."	"<b>Diastereomers&nbsp;</b>differ in <i>configuration </i>at <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>some</i> (<u>not</u> all)</span> chiral centers.

"<div>In optical activity, the symbol for the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;observed&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[observed/specific]</span>&nbsp;rotation is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>α</b></span>.<br></div>"	"<div>In optical activity, the symbol for the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>observed</i></span>&nbsp;rotation is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>α</b></span>.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Specific rotation is [α]<sub>D</sub>. Equation shown below:<div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-024aa8576e2630192b79da8ecf4004cdeb08a9c8.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""1b806a2579d20672b92fd6151b137617.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">Enantiomers</b></span>&nbsp;<i>always&nbsp;</i>have <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;the&#x20;same&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[the same/different]</span> <u>physical</u>&nbsp;&amp; <u>chemical</u>&nbsp;properties, including density, boiling point, melting point, reactivity, etc."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">Enantiomers</b></span>&nbsp;<i>always&nbsp;</i>have <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>the same</i></span> <u>physical</u>&nbsp;&amp; <u>chemical</u>&nbsp;properties, including density, boiling point, melting point, reactivity, etc.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Diastereomers have different physical/chemical properties.</div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>Diastereomers</u></span> <i>always </i>have <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;different&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[the same/different]</span> <b>physical&nbsp;</b>&amp;<b> chemical</b><b>&nbsp;properties</b>, including density, boiling point, melting point, reactivity, etc.<br></div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>Diastereomers</u></span> <i>always </i>have <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>different</i></span> <b>physical&nbsp;</b>&amp;<b> chemical</b><b>&nbsp;properties</b>, including density, boiling point, melting point, reactivity, etc.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- Density, boiling point, melting point, solubilities will be different.<div>- This is why diastereomers can be separated by fractional distillation, extraction, etc.</div></div>"
"<div>In terms of optical activity, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;specific&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[specific/observed]</span> <b>rotation </b>is the <i>direction </i>(+ or -)<i>&nbsp;</i>polarized light is rotated under <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>standard</u></span> conditions.</div>"	"<div>In terms of optical activity, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>specific</b></span> <b>rotation </b>is the <i>direction </i>(+ or -)<i>&nbsp;</i>polarized light is rotated under <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>standard</u></span> conditions.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Just a way to <i>standardize </i>optical activity.<div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-024aa8576e2630192b79da8ecf4004cdeb08a9c8.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""ec8d40ebee8a7a7da04487190d13533e.png""><br></div></div>"

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"<div><b>Meso </b>compounds are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""achiral"" data-ordinal=""1"">[chiral/achiral]</span> due to their internal plane of symmetry.<br></div>"	"<div><b>Meso </b>compounds are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">achiral</span> due to their internal plane of symmetry.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Meso compounds are <i>achiral</i><div><i><br></i></div><div><img src=""c3e162846d986b27e6577434eb2b4b13.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""Picture24-2.png""></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;Meso&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> compounds are achiral compounds with an internal <u style="""">plane of symmetry</u>&nbsp;&amp; an <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">even</span> number of chiral centers."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">Meso</b></span> compounds are achiral compounds with an internal <u style="""">plane of symmetry</u>&nbsp;&amp; an <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">even</span> number of chiral centers.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Meso compounds are <i>achiral</i>&nbsp;since their even number of chiral centers cancel each other out (equal number of S's to R's).<br></div><div><i><br></i></div><div><img src=""c3e162846d986b27e6577434eb2b4b13.png""><i><br></i></div><div><i><br></i></div><div><img src=""Picture24-2.png""><i><br></i></div></div>"
"<div><b>Meso </b>compounds <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;do&#x20;not&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[do/do not]</span> have <u>enantiomers</u> or <u>diastereomers</u>.<br></div>"	"<div><b>Meso </b>compounds <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>do not</i></span> have <u>enantiomers</u> or <u>diastereomers</u>.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...since meso compounds are achiral.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""c3e162846d986b27e6577434eb2b4b13.png""><br></div><div><img src=""Picture24-2.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div><b>Meso</b> compounds are optically <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;inactive&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[active/inactive]</span> since their even number of chiral centers <i>cancel </i>each other out.</div>"	"<div><b>Meso</b> compounds are optically <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>inactive</i></span> since their even number of chiral centers <i>cancel </i>each other out.</div>

</div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Low"" data-ordinal=""1"">[high/low]</span> pK<sub>a</sub> values indicate <i>stronger </i>acids."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Low</span> pK<sub>a</sub> values indicate <i>stronger </i>acids.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"<div><b>Electron </b><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;withdrawing&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> groups <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>increase</i></span> acidity &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>decrease</i></span> basicity on compounds.</div>"	"<div><b>Electron </b><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">withdrawing</b></span> groups <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>increase</i></span> acidity &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>decrease</i></span> basicity on compounds.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Electron withdrawing groups weaken the bonds holding onto hydrogen.<div>-&nbsp;<b>Bases</b>: strongest when they're more negative (more electrons)</div></div></div>"
"<b>Electron&nbsp;</b><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;donating&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> groups <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>decrease</i></span> acidity &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>increase</i></span> basicity on compounds."	"<b>Electron&nbsp;</b><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">donating</b></span> groups <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>decrease</i></span> acidity &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>increase</i></span> basicity on compounds.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- <b>Acids</b>: donating electrons makes it less likely to give away its proton (since its ""hold"" is stronger).<div>- <b>Bases</b>: makes it more likely to attack an electrophile as a Lewis base.</div></div>"
"<b>NO<sub style="""">2</sub></b> and <b>halides</b> are electron <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""withdrawing"" data-ordinal=""1"">[withdrawing/donating]</span> groups."	"<b>NO<sub style="""">2</sub></b> and <b>halides</b> are electron <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">withdrawing</span> groups.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;Increase acidity</div></div>"
"<b>NH<sub style="""">2</sub></b> and -<b>O-CH<sub style="""">3</sub></b>&nbsp;are electron <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;donating&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[withdrawing/donating]</span>."	"<b>NH<sub style="""">2</sub></b> and -<b>O-CH<sub style="""">3</sub></b>&nbsp;are electron <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>donating</i></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Decrease&nbsp;acidity &amp; increases basicity.</div></div>"
"<u>Carboxylic acids</u>&nbsp;are electron <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""withdrawing"" data-ordinal=""1"">[withdrawing/donating]</span>."	"<u>Carboxylic acids</u>&nbsp;are electron <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">withdrawing</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><i>Increase&nbsp;</i>acidity</div></div>"

<div>0° of specific rotation, since the two enantiomers are exactly 50/50 in the solution.</div><div></div></div>"
"S enantiomers have the same <i>magnitude</i>&nbsp;(different <i>direction</i>)&nbsp;of specific rotation<i>&nbsp;</i>as R enantiomers. Is this true for <i><u>diastereomers</u> </i>as well?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;No&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&#x2C;&#x20;you&#x20;&lt;i&gt;cannot&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;automatically&#x20;know&#x20;the&#x20;magnitude&#x20;if&#x20;you&#x27;re&#x20;given&#x20;just&#x20;one&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"S enantiomers have the same <i>magnitude</i>&nbsp;(different <i>direction</i>)&nbsp;of specific rotation<i>&nbsp;</i>as R enantiomers. Is this true for <i><u>diastereomers</u> </i>as well?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>No</b>, you <i>cannot </i>automatically know the magnitude if you're given just one.</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Enantiomers → S &amp; R have equal magnitudes in opposite directions.</div><div>- Diastereomers → magnitude might be different.</div><div></div></div>"
"When should <u style="""">E/Z notation</u> be used instead of cis/trans to describe a molecule's configuration?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""When&#x20;the&#x20;double&#x20;bond&#x20;has&#x20;&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;2&#x20;or&#x20;more&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&#x20;substituents"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"When should <u style="""">E/Z notation</u> be used instead of cis/trans to describe a molecule's configuration?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">When the double bond has <b style="""">2 or more</b> substituents</span></div>

...since hydrogens bond via s-orbitals.<div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-fbcd16ea7aba666e2ae02a342a530119c87e4bd3.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"Bonds with <i>larger </i><b>overlapping orbitals</b>&nbsp;(ex: sp<sup>3</sup>-sp<sup>3</sup>) are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;longer&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[longer/shorter]</span> in radius."	"Bonds with <i>larger </i><b>overlapping orbitals</b>&nbsp;(ex: sp<sup>3</sup>-sp<sup>3</sup>) are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i><u>longer</u></i></span> in radius.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...meaning sp<sup>3</sup>-sp<sup>3</sup> bonds are <i>longer</i> than only s-sp<sup>3</sup> bonds.</div><div><br></div><img src=""553abf8033d43e223930ea4f0a679c2b.png""></div>"
"Why does Cl<sub>2</sub> release <i>more </i>energy than <b>F<sub>2</sub></b> when its bond is broken?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;F&lt;sub&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&#x20;only&#x20;has&#x20;&lt;i&gt;only&#x20;1&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;single&#x20;&lt;b&gt;π&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&#x20;bond&#x20;with&#x20;&lt;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;no&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;σ&#x20;bonds&#x2E;&#x20;π&#x20;bonds&#x20;&lt;i&gt;do&#x20;not&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;release&#x20;as&#x20;much&#x20;energy&#x20;as&#x20;σ&#x20;bonds&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"Why does Cl<sub>2</sub> release <i>more </i>energy than <b>F<sub>2</sub></b> when its bond is broken?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>F<sub>2</sub></b> only has <i>only 1</i> single <b>π</b> bond with <i><u>no</u></i> σ bonds. π bonds <i>do not</i> release as much energy as σ bonds.</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""Periodic_Table.png""><br></div></div>"
"<b>Electron densities</b> can <i>only</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>overlap</u></span> when their electrons have the <i>same</i>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""spin&#x20;directions"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"<b>Electron densities</b> can <i>only</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>overlap</u></span> when their electrons have the <i>same</i>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">spin directions</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"<b>Electron densities</b> can <i>only</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;overlap&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> when their electrons have the <i>same</i>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">spin directions</span>."	"<b>Electron densities</b> can <i>only</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>overlap</u></span> when their electrons have the <i>same</i>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">spin directions</span>.

</div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Bonding&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[bonding/antibonding]</span> molecular orbitals are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>lower</i></span> in <u>energy</u>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Bonding</b></span> molecular orbitals are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>lower</i></span> in <u>energy</u>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Bonding</b>&nbsp;orbitals → most of the electron density resides between the two nuclei - making it more stable &amp; lower in energy.</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Antibonding</b>&nbsp;orbitals → most of the electron density lies outside of the two nuclei - making it less stable &amp; higher in energy.</div></div><div><br></div><img src=""0f9af0b773022613f0122fe25f0f74fe.png""></div>"

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Can go <b>beyond</b> just 8 electrons.</div><div>- Ex: phosphorus has 10 valence electrons here.</div><div><br></div><img src=""03.16.161393641880858091395996571982560202530.jpg""><br></div>"
"<i>Why </i>do atoms form <b>hybridized orbitals</b> in the first place?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Following&#x20;Hund&#x27;s&#x20;rule&#x2C;&#x20;it&#x20;&lt;b&gt;&lt;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;allows&#x20;the&#x20;atom&#x20;to&#x20;bond&#x20;with&#x20;more&#x20;atoms&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"<i>Why </i>do atoms form <b>hybridized orbitals</b> in the first place?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Following Hund's rule, it <b><i><u>allows the atom to bond with more atoms</u></i></b>.</span><br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>If you tried filling the regular orbitals... 2 arrows would be in 2s, leaving the other 2 for 2p. That would only allow 3 bonds to be formed.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""802c5069e1ca3cf859ae18047011f140.png""></div><div><a href=""https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vHXViZTxLXo"">https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vHXViZTxLXo</a><br></div></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;sp&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>-hybridized orbitals are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">linear</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">180</span>°.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>sp</b></span>-hybridized orbitals are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">linear</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">180</span>°.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""paste-fbcd16ea7aba666e2ae02a342a530119c87e4bd3.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>sp</b></span>-hybridized orbitals are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""linear"" data-ordinal=""2"">[shape]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""180"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>°.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>sp</b></span>-hybridized orbitals are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">linear</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">180</span>°.</div>

"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;sp&lt;sup&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>-hybridized orbitals are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">trigonal planar</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">120</span>°.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>sp<sup>2</sup></b></span>-hybridized orbitals are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">trigonal planar</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">120</span>°.</div>

"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>sp<sup>2</sup></b></span>-hybridized orbitals are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""trigonal&#x20;planar"" data-ordinal=""2"">[shape]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""120"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>°.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>sp<sup>2</sup></b></span>-hybridized orbitals are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">trigonal planar</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">120</span>°.</div>

"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;sp&lt;sup&gt;3&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>-hybridized orbitals are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">tetrahedral</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">109.5</span>°.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>sp<sup>3</sup></b></span>-hybridized orbitals are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">tetrahedral</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">109.5</span>°.</div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>In order to be bent, you don't need to have exactly 2 lone pairs (4 electrons total) - you just <u>need to have lone pairs</u>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""e30282d2b6d86da7873aa5da609a5685.png""><br></div></div>"
"<b>sp<sup>3</sup></b>-hybridized atoms with <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""2"" data-ordinal=""2"">[#]</span> bonds &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""2"" data-ordinal=""2"">[#]</span> lone pairs are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>bent</u></span>."	"<b>sp<sup>3</sup></b>-hybridized atoms with <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">2</span> bonds &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">2</span> lone pairs are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>bent</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>In order to be bent, you don't need to have exactly 2 lone pairs (4 electrons total) - you just <u>need to have lone pairs</u>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""e30282d2b6d86da7873aa5da609a5685.png""><br></div></div>"
"<b style="""">sp<sup style="""">3</sup></b>-hybridized atoms with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">4</span> atoms &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">0</span> lone pairs are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;tetrahedral&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[shape]</span>."	"<b style="""">sp<sup style="""">3</sup></b>-hybridized atoms with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">4</span> atoms &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">0</span> lone pairs are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">tetrahedral</u></span>.

"<div>Equation to calculate the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;&lt;i&gt;non&#x2D;standard&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;enthalpy&#x20;of&#x20;a&#x20;reaction&#x20;&#x28;ΔH&lt;sub&gt;rnx&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>:<br></div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""1206a5f2c33d4e1a949542e31544ea5f.png""></span><br></div>"	"<div>Equation to calculate the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b><i>non-standard</i> enthalpy of a reaction (ΔH<sub>rnx</sub>)</b></span>:<br></div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""1206a5f2c33d4e1a949542e31544ea5f.png""></span><br></div>

<script>
// Prevent original image from loading before mask
aFade = 50, qFade = 0;
var mask = document.querySelector('#io-overlay>img');
function loaded() {
    var original = document.querySelector('#io-original');
    original.style.visibility = ""visible"";
}
if (mask === null || mask.complete) {
    loaded();
} else {
    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}
</script>

// Toggle answer mask on clicking the image
var toggle = function() {
  var amask = document.getElementById('io-overlay');
  if (amask.style.display === 'block' || amask.style.display === '')
    amask.style.display = 'none';
  else
    amask.style.display = 'block'
}

<script>
// Prevent original image from loading before mask
aFade = 50, qFade = 0;
var mask = document.querySelector('#io-overlay>img');
function loaded() {
    var original = document.querySelector('#io-original');
    original.style.visibility = ""visible"";
}
if (mask === null || mask.complete) {
    loaded();
} else {
    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}
</script>

  if (amask.style.display === 'block' || amask.style.display === '')
    amask.style.display = 'none';
  else
    amask.style.display = 'block'
}

<div><b><u>Acidity</u></b>...<br></div><div><b>sp</b>&nbsp;&gt; sp<sup>2</sup>&nbsp;&gt; sp<sup>3</sup></div><div><br></div><img src=""d73f74dd58e6b008869dad77b565fa61.png""></div>"
"The&nbsp;<i>more</i>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>π</b></span>-bonds a compound has, the&nbsp;<i>more </i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;acidic&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[acidic/basic]</span>."	"The&nbsp;<i>more</i>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>π</b></span>-bonds a compound has, the&nbsp;<i>more </i><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i><u>acidic</u></i></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b><u>Acidity</u></b>...<br></div><div><b>sp</b>&nbsp;&gt; sp<sup>2</sup>&nbsp;&gt; sp<sup>3</sup></div><div><br></div><img src=""d73f74dd58e6b008869dad77b565fa61.png""></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>inductive</b></span> <b>effect </b>is when <u>electrons</u> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>withdrawn</i></span> or <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>donated</i></span> across <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;σ&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[σ/π]</span> bonds."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>inductive</b></span> <b>effect </b>is when <u>electrons</u> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>withdrawn</i></span> or <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>donated</i></span> across <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>σ</b></span> bonds.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""c015e1d62ef7bdbe62b88ba099a8609c.png""></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;inductive&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> <b>effect </b>is when <u>electrons</u> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>withdrawn</i></span> or <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>donated</i></span> across <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>σ</b></span> bonds."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>inductive</b></span> <b>effect </b>is when <u>electrons</u> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>withdrawn</i></span> or <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>donated</i></span> across <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>σ</b></span> bonds.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""c015e1d62ef7bdbe62b88ba099a8609c.png""></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>inductive</b></span> <b>effect </b>is when <u>electrons</u> are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;withdrawn&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span> or <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;donated&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span> across <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>σ</b></span> bonds."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>inductive</b></span> <b>effect </b>is when <u>electrons</u> are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>withdrawn</i></span> or <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>donated</i></span> across <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>σ</b></span> bonds.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""c015e1d62ef7bdbe62b88ba099a8609c.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Fluorine&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is the <i>most </i><u>electronegative</u> atom."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Fluorine</b></span> is the <i>most </i><u>electronegative</u> atom.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Increases <i><u>up</u></i> &amp; to the <u><i>right</i></u>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""Periodic_Table.png""></div>"
"Electron <u style="""">withdrawing</u> groups <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;decrease&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[increase/decrease]</span> <b>nucleophilicity</b>."	"Electron <u style="""">withdrawing</u> groups <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>decrease</i></span> <b>nucleophilicity</b>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""c015e1d62ef7bdbe62b88ba099a8609c.png""></div>"
"Electron <u style="""">withdrawing</u> groups <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;increase&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[increase/decrease]</span> <b>electrophilicity</b>."	"Electron <u style="""">withdrawing</u> groups <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>increase</i></span> <b>electrophilicity</b>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Makes them <i>easier </i>to get attacked (by making them more positively charged)</div><div><br></div><img src=""c015e1d62ef7bdbe62b88ba099a8609c.png""></div>"
"Electron <u>donating</u>&nbsp;groups <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;decrease&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[increase/decrease]</span> <b>electrophilicity</b>."	"Electron <u>donating</u>&nbsp;groups <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i><u>decrease</u></i></span> <b>electrophilicity</b>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Electrophiles have strong partial positive charges, which cause nucleophiles (with strong negatives) to attack.</div><div><br></div><img src=""c015e1d62ef7bdbe62b88ba099a8609c.png""></div>"
"Electron <u>donating</u>&nbsp;groups <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;increase&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[increase/decrease]</span> <b>nucleophilicity</b>."	"Electron <u>donating</u>&nbsp;groups <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>increase</i></span> <b>nucleophilicity</b>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""c015e1d62ef7bdbe62b88ba099a8609c.png""></div>"
"<b style="""">CH<sub style="""">3</sub></b> groups are electron <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;donating&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[withdrawing/donating]</span>."	"<b style="""">CH<sub style="""">3</sub></b> groups are electron <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">donating</i></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"A compound's <b>boiling point</b> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">increases</i></span> when its number<i> </i>of <u>intermolecular forces</u> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;increases&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[increases/decreases]</span>."	"A compound's <b>boiling point</b> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">increases</i></span> when its number<i> </i>of <u>intermolecular forces</u> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>increases</i></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- The stronger the bonds are between individual molecules in solution, the harder it is for them to ""escape"" as gases.</div><div>- Hydrogen bonding plays a more significant role than Van der Waal stackability in increasing the boiling point.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-f1857e054fa71577a137bb78926e78d554df7938.jpg""></div>"
"A compound's <b>boiling point</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;increases&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[increases/decreases]</span> when its number<i> </i>of <u>intermolecular forces</u> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>increases</i></span>."	"A compound's <b>boiling point</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">increases</i></span> when its number<i> </i>of <u>intermolecular forces</u> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>increases</i></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- The stronger the bonds are between individual molecules in solution, the harder it is for them to ""escape"" as gases.</div><div>- Hydrogen bonding plays a more significant role than Van der Waal stackability in increasing the boiling point.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-f1857e054fa71577a137bb78926e78d554df7938.jpg""></div>"
"<u>Hydrogen bonds</u> are <i>strongest</i> when the <b>H</b> atom is <i>bound </i>to the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;more&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[more/less]</span> <b>electronegative</b> atom."	"<u>Hydrogen bonds</u> are <i>strongest</i> when the <b>H</b> atom is <i>bound </i>to the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i><u>more</u></i></span> <b>electronegative</b> atom.

"Molecules with <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>high</i></span> <b>molecular weights</b> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>harder</i></span> to melt or vaporize.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Amphoteric</b></span> <b>molecules </b>are those that can act as <i>both </i>Bronsted-Lowry <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;acids&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>&nbsp;<i>and</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;bases&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Amphoteric</b></span> <b>molecules </b>are those that can act as <i>both </i>Bronsted-Lowry <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>acids</u></span>&nbsp;<i>and</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>bases</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Water is amphoteric<div><br></div><div><img src=""430-4308673_the-water-molecule-could-act-as-a-base.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Amphoteric&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> <b>molecules </b>are those that can act as <i>both </i>Bronsted-Lowry <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>acids</u></span>&nbsp;<i>and</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>bases</u></span>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Amphoteric</b></span> <b>molecules </b>are those that can act as <i>both </i>Bronsted-Lowry <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>acids</u></span>&nbsp;<i>and</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>bases</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Water is amphoteric<div><br></div><div><img src=""430-4308673_the-water-molecule-could-act-as-a-base.png""><br></div></div>"
"<b>Water </b>acts a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>base</i></span> in <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;acidic&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[acidic/basic]</span> solutions but acts as an <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>acid</i></span> in <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;basic&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[acidic/basic]</span> solutions."	"<b>Water </b>acts a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>base</i></span> in <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>acidic</u></span> solutions but acts as an <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>acid</i></span> in <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>basic</u></span> solutions.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Its oxygen can <u>accept</u> hydrogens, but it's not capable itself of donating hydrogens.</div><div><br></div><img src=""6c70f3e8ce8fa1566173fdd66d3416a6.png""></div>"
"<div>Reactions involving <b>nucleophiles </b>&amp; <b>electrophiles </b><i>always</i> use <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;polar&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[polar/non-polar]</span> solvents.</div>"	"<div>Reactions involving <b>nucleophiles </b>&amp; <b>electrophiles </b><i>always</i> use <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i><u>polar</u></i></span> solvents.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...since polar solvents are required to <i>dissolve</i> nucleophiles &amp; electrophiles. <i>Every </i>reaction is polar, but <i>not </i>every reaction is protic (hydrogen bonds).</div></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Nucleophilic</b>/<b>electrophilic</b></span>&nbsp;reactions favor the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;kinetic&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[thermodynamic/kinetic]</span> products of a reaction - whichever product <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""forms&#x20;the&#x20;&lt;i&gt;fastest&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Nucleophilic</b>/<b>electrophilic</b></span>&nbsp;reactions favor the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>kinetic</u></span> products of a reaction - whichever product <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">forms the <i>fastest</i></span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Nucleophilic/electrophilic</b> reactions → favor <u>kinetic</u> products.</div><div>- <b>Acid/base</b> reactions → favor&nbsp;<u>thermodynamic</u> products.</div><div><br></div><img src=""5aac12fc1c699894933423ee7ad06634.png""></div>"
"<b style="""">Electrophiles </b>act like Lewis <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""acids"" data-ordinal=""1"">[acids/bases]</span> since they <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;accept&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[accept/donate]</span> electrons."	"<b style="""">Electrophiles </b>act like Lewis <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">acids</span> since they <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>accept</i></span> electrons.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""5aac12fc1c699894933423ee7ad06634.png""></div>"
"<b style="""">Carbonyl carbons</b> are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;better&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[better/worse]</span> <u>electrophiles</u> than partially positive carbons."	"<b style="""">Carbonyl carbons</b> are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>better</i></span> <u>electrophiles</u> than partially positive carbons.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...because they're being made even more positive by the lone pair above.</div><div><br></div><img src=""5aac12fc1c699894933423ee7ad06634.png""></div>"
"<b>Carbonyl carbons</b> are strong <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;electrophiles&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[nuc/elec]</span> due to their strong <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;positive&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[+/-]</span> charges from nearby electron-withdrawing groups."	"<b>Carbonyl carbons</b> are strong <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">electrophiles</u></span> due to their strong <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">positive</i></span> charges from nearby electron-withdrawing groups.
<hr>

"Strong <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b><i>electrophiles</i></b></span> are strong <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;font&#x2D;style&#x3A;&#x20;italic&#x3B;&quot;&gt;acids&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[acids/bases]</span>."	"Strong <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b><i>electrophiles</i></b></span> are strong <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style=""font-style: italic;"">acids</u></span>.

<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Usually weak bases.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""f978f44977ff317bde52633a0947df38.png""><br></div></div>"
"<b>Leaving groups</b> are broken off via <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;hetero&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[homo/hetero]</span><u style="""">lytic</u> cleavages."	"<b>Leaving groups</b> are broken off via <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">hetero</u></span><u style="""">lytic</u> cleavages.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...since the leaving group <b><u>takes <i>both</i> electrons</u></b>.</div><div>- <b>Heterolytic</b> cleavage: one atom <u>takes both</u> electrons.</div><div>- <b>Homolytic</b> cleavage: split <u>evenly</u> between the two.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""f978f44977ff317bde52633a0947df38.png""><br></div></div>"
"The <i>best </i><b>leaving groups</b> are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;weak&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[weak/strong]</span> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;bases&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[acids/bases]</span>, since they best&nbsp;<i>stabilize </i>negative charges."	"The <i>best </i><b>leaving groups</b> are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">weak</i></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>bases</u></span>, since they best&nbsp;<i>stabilize </i>negative charges.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Weak bases are only slightly negative (best stability), whereas strong bases are highly negative (not stable at all).</div><div><br></div><div>- Conjugate bases of strong acids are the best leaving groups.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""f978f44977ff317bde52633a0947df38.png""><br></div></div>"
"In order for <b>nucleophilic attacks</b> to happen, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""products"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> <i>must </i>be <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i><u>more stable</u></i></span> than the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""reactants"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"In order for <b>nucleophilic attacks</b> to happen, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">products</span> <i>must </i>be <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i><u>more stable</u></i></span> than the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">reactants</span>.

"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>S<sub>N</sub>2</b></span> reactions are limited <i>both </i>by the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>nucleophile</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>leaving group</u></span>.

<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...because a stable carbocation can be formed.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""ce772f9765ff2ebcb1f4b3be2bff22bd.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-4cbc563435027b03e93df2e5e588a8c0c5575b16.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"Rate equation for&nbsp;<b>S<sub>N</sub>2</b>&nbsp;reactions...<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""rate&#x20;&#x3D;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;k&#x5B;substrate&#x5D;&#x5B;nucleophile&#x5D;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"Rate equation for&nbsp;<b>S<sub>N</sub>2</b>&nbsp;reactions...<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">rate =&nbsp;k[substrate][nucleophile]</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Relies both on the strength of the nucleophile &amp; the substrate (the ease at which the substrate's leaving group leaves), since both of these things act at the same time.</div><div><br></div><img src=""797829fece8dbb953eb5208c9ee4ade7.png""></div>"
"<b>CO<sub>2</sub></b> is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;more&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[more/less]</span> <b>oxidized </b>than carboxylic acids or anhydrides."	"<b>CO<sub>2</sub></b> is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i><u>more</u></i></span> <b>oxidized </b>than carboxylic acids or anhydrides.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""ImagesHandler (2).ashx""></div>"
"Good <b style="""">oxidizing agents</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;accept&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[accept/donate]</span> electrons from other molecules."	"Good <b style="""">oxidizing agents</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>accept</i></span> electrons from other molecules.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Help other compounds get oxidized by ""holding onto"" their electrons for them.</div>- Mn<sup>7+</sup> &amp; Cr<sup>6+</sup>&nbsp;are great oxidizing agents due to their <i>unusually high</i> affinity for electrons (""lots of positives"")<div><br></div><div><img src=""5dde3a3a5eb0c9bd96a9602eb74c606f.png""><br></div></div>"
"<b>CrO<sub>3</sub> </b>is a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;strong&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[weak/strong]</span> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;oxidant&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[oxidant/reducing agent]</span>."	"<b>CrO<sub>3</sub> </b>is a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>strong</i></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>oxidant</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""5dde3a3a5eb0c9bd96a9602eb74c606f.png""></div>"

"<b>Na</b><sub style=""font-weight: bold;"">2</sub><b>Cr</b><sub style=""font-weight: bold;"">2</sub><b>O</b><sub style=""""><b>7</b>&nbsp;</sub>&amp; <b>K<sub>2</sub>Cr<sub>2</sub>O<sub>7</sub>&nbsp;</b>are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i><u>strong</u></i></span> oxidants.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Oxidize <i>all the way</i> to <u>carboxylic acids</u>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""5dde3a3a5eb0c9bd96a9602eb74c606f.png""></div>"
"<b>PCC&nbsp;</b>is a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;weak&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[weak/strong]</span> oxidant."	"<b>PCC&nbsp;</b>is a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i><u>weak</u></i></span> oxidant.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Can't oxidize all the way to a carboxylic acid from an alcohol... has to do it <u>incrementally</u>.</div><div>- It's unable to oxidize straight to an alcohol because it's anhydrous (doesn't have water). Water is needed by strong oxidants to protonate the aldehyde into a hydrate (necessary intermediate).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""5dde3a3a5eb0c9bd96a9602eb74c606f.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;Oxidizing&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[oxidizing/reducing]</span> <b style="""">agents</b>&nbsp;are typically <u>metals</u>&nbsp;bound to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>oxygen</u></span> atoms."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">Oxidizing</b></span> <b style="""">agents</b>&nbsp;are typically <u>metals</u>&nbsp;bound to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>oxygen</u></span> atoms.

<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- The low electronegativity allows the reducing agents to easily pass off electrons.</div><div>- Ex: Na, Mg, Al, Zn</div><div>- Reducing agents get oxidized themselves (lose electrons easily).</div><div><br></div><img src=""8cb3ef2c154eda46f38bc3859136c7bf.png""></div>"

"Good <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">reducing</b></span> <b style="""">agents</b> have atoms with <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>low</i></span> <u>electronegativities</u>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>S<sub>N</sub>1</b></span> reactions <i>prefer </i><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">tertiary 3°</span> carbons, due to the <u>carbocation stability</u>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>In contrast, <b>standard enthalpy ΔH°</b> is calculated by subtracting (product's ΔH°)&nbsp;- (reactant's ΔH°)</div><div><br></div><img src=""1206a5f2c33d4e1a949542e31544ea5f.png""></div>"
"<div>Substitution reactions at areas with <i>lots </i>of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;steric&#x20;hinderance&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>&nbsp;occur as <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>S<sub>N</sub>1</b></span> reactions.<br></div>"	"<div>Substitution reactions at areas with <i>lots </i>of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>steric hinderance</u></span>&nbsp;occur as <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>S<sub>N</sub>1</b></span> reactions.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...because a stable carbocation can be formed.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""ce772f9765ff2ebcb1f4b3be2bff22bd.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-4cbc563435027b03e93df2e5e588a8c0c5575b16.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"On the periodic table, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;acidity&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""4"">[...]</span>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i><u>increases</u></i></span> as you move <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">down</span> &amp; to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">right</span> due to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i><u>weaker bonding</u></i></span>."	"On the periodic table, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""4""><b>acidity</b></span>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i><u>increases</u></i></span> as you move <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">down</span> &amp; to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">right</span> due to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i><u>weaker bonding</u></i></span>.

"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Hetero&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><b>lytic&nbsp;</b>cleavage is when the electrons are shared <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>un</i><i style="""">equally</i></u></span> after the bond is broken.<br></div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Hetero</b></span><b>lytic&nbsp;</b>cleavage is when the electrons are shared <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>un</i><i style="""">equally</i></u></span> after the bond is broken.<br></div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>One atom gets both.</div><div><br></div><img src=""fissionsummary.png""></div>"
"Requirements for <b>hydrogen bonding</b>:<div><br></div><div>1<sup>st</sup> molecule: <i>hydrogen </i>is attached to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;F&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;O&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>, or <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;N&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div><div>2<sup>nd</sup> molecule: has a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;lone&#x20;pair&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> on F, O, or N.</div>"	"Requirements for <b>hydrogen bonding</b>:<div><br></div><div>1<sup>st</sup> molecule: <i>hydrogen </i>is attached to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>F</u></span>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>O</u></span>, or <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>N</u></span></div><div>2<sup>nd</sup> molecule: has a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>lone pair</u></span> on F, O, or N.</div>

<div><img src=""9be38d7820e345e8d1a41dc4b2f04531.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div><b>Electron </b><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">withdrawing</b></span> groups <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;increase&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[inc/dec]</span> acidity &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;decrease&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[inc/dec]</span> basicity on compounds.</div>"	"<div><b>Electron </b><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">withdrawing</b></span> groups <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>increase</i></span> acidity &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>decrease</i></span> basicity on compounds.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Electron withdrawing groups weaken the bonds holding onto hydrogen.<div>-&nbsp;<b>Bases</b>: strongest when they're more negative (more electrons)</div></div></div>"
"<b>Electron&nbsp;</b><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">donating</b></span> groups <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;decrease&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[inc/dec]</span> acidity &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;increase&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[inc/dec]</span> basicity on compounds."	"<b>Electron&nbsp;</b><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">donating</b></span> groups <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>decrease</i></span> acidity &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>increase</i></span> basicity on compounds.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- <b>Acids</b>: donating electrons makes it less likely to give away its proton (since its ""hold"" is stronger).<div>- <b>Bases</b>: makes it more likely to attack an electrophile as a Lewis base.</div></div>"
"Rank the <u>acidity</u>&nbsp;(<i>most </i>to <i>least</i>) of <b>phenol</b> substituent positions (<i>ortho/meta/para</i>)...<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Ortho"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>- <b>&gt;</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""para"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>- <b>&gt;</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""meta"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>-</div>"	"Rank the <u>acidity</u>&nbsp;(<i>most </i>to <i>least</i>) of <b>phenol</b> substituent positions (<i>ortho/meta/para</i>)...<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Ortho</span>- <b>&gt;</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">para</span>- <b>&gt;</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">meta</span>-</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Ortho &amp; para positions are always the most significant on phenols because only these spots can bear a negative charge.</div><div>&nbsp; &nbsp;&nbsp;</div><div>&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp;- Ortho: highest inductive effect (closest) - most acidic</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""a8932b26ae012069de04b371679f04d4.png""><br></div></div>"
"Why is the <b><u>ortho</u></b>- position the <i>best</i> for <u>electron withdrawing</u> groups for <b>phenol <i>acidity</i></b>?<br><br><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Allows&#x20;it&#x20;to&#x20;be&#x20;&lt;i&gt;closest&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;to&#x20;the&#x20;alcohol&#x2C;&#x20;so&#x20;it&#x20;has&#x20;a&#x20;&lt;u&gt;higher&#x20;inductive&#x20;effect&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"Why is the <b><u>ortho</u></b>- position the <i>best</i> for <u>electron withdrawing</u> groups for <b>phenol <i>acidity</i></b>?<br><br><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Allows it to be <i>closest </i>to the alcohol, so it has a <u>higher inductive effect</u>.</span><br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Ortho &amp; para positions are always the most significant on phenols because only these spots can bear a negative charge.</div><div>&nbsp; &nbsp;&nbsp;</div><div>&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp;- Ortho: highest inductive effect (closest) - most acidic</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""a8932b26ae012069de04b371679f04d4.png""></div></div>"
"Equation for the <i>number</i> of carbons &amp; hydrogens&nbsp;on a(n) <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>straight-chain alkane</u></span> with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">0</span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">double or triple</span> bonds:<br><br><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""C&lt;sub&gt;n&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;H&lt;sub&gt;2n&#x2B;2&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"Equation for the <i>number</i> of carbons &amp; hydrogens&nbsp;on a(n) <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>straight-chain alkane</u></span> with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">0</span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">double or triple</span> bonds:<br><br><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">C<sub>n</sub>H<sub>2n+2</sub></span><br></div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Gas chromatography also separates compounds by their boiling points, but the compounds are in their gas phases.</div><div><br></div><img src=""simpledist.gif""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Distillation</b></span> separates <i>two </i><u>liquids</u> from each other by differences in their <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""boiling&#x20;points"" data-ordinal=""2"">[property]</span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Distillation</b></span> separates <i>two </i><u>liquids</u> from each other by differences in their <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">boiling points</span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Gas chromatography also separates compounds by their boiling points, but the compounds are in their gas phases.</div><div><br></div><img src=""simpledist.gif""></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Volatility&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is the <i>ease </i>at which a substance can <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>evaporate</u></span>.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Volatility</b></span> is the <i>ease </i>at which a substance can <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>evaporate</u></span>.</div>

"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Volatility</b></span> is the <i>ease </i>at which a substance can <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;evaporate&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Volatility</b></span> is the <i>ease </i>at which a substance can <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>evaporate</u></span>.</div>

"Compounds with <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;low&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[high/low]</span> <u>boiling points</u> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">more</i></span> <b>volatile </b>."	"Compounds with <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">low</i></span> <u>boiling points</u> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">more</i></span> <b>volatile </b>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>This is used in distillation to separate two liquids from each other.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""simpledist.gif""><br></div></div>"
"Compounds with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">low</i></span> <u>boiling points</u> are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;more&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[more/less]</span> <b>volatile </b>."	"Compounds with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">low</i></span> <u>boiling points</u> are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">more</i></span> <b>volatile </b>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>This is used in distillation to separate two liquids from each other.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""simpledist.gif""><br></div></div>"
"In <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;distillation&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[lab technique]</span>, the compound with the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">lower</i></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>boiling point</u></span> will vaporize <i>first</i> and travel into the distillation column."	"In <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>distillation</b></span>, the compound with the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">lower</i></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>boiling point</u></span> will vaporize <i>first</i> and travel into the distillation column.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"In <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>distillation</b></span>, the compound with the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;lower&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[higher/lower]</span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>boiling point</u></span> will vaporize <i>first</i> and travel into the distillation column."	"In <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>distillation</b></span>, the compound with the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">lower</i></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>boiling point</u></span> will vaporize <i>first</i> and travel into the distillation column.

"In <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>distillation</b></span>, the compound with the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">lower</i></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>boiling point</u></span> will vaporize <i>first</i> and travel into the distillation column.

"In <b>distillation</b>, the vaporized compound becomes <i>more </i>purified after <i>each</i> round of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>condensation</u>&nbsp;&amp;&nbsp;<u>re-evaporation</u></span> in the column.

"In <b>distillation</b>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;higher&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[higher/lower]</span> temperatures result in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>more</i></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>condensation &amp; evaporation</u></span> in the distilling column."	"In <b>distillation</b>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">higher</i></span> temperatures result in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>more</i></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>condensation &amp; evaporation</u></span> in the distilling column.

<div>Purifies the compound.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""simpledist.gif""><br></div></div>"
"In <b>distillation</b>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">higher</i></span> temperatures result in <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;more&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[more/less]</span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>condensation &amp; evaporation</u></span> in the distilling column."	"In <b>distillation</b>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">higher</i></span> temperatures result in <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>more</i></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>condensation &amp; evaporation</u></span> in the distilling column.

"In <b>distillation</b>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">higher</i></span> temperatures result in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>more</i></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;condensation&#x20;&amp;amp&#x3B;&#x20;evaporation&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span> in the distilling column."	"In <b>distillation</b>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">higher</i></span> temperatures result in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>more</i></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>condensation &amp; evaporation</u></span> in the distilling column.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Purifies the compound.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""simpledist.gif""><br></div></div>"
"Water molecules with the <i>greatest </i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;kinetic&#x20;energy&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> &amp;&nbsp;<i>closest </i>to the liquid's <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;surface&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> have the highest chances of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>vaporizing</b></span>."	"Water molecules with the <i>greatest </i><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>kinetic energy</u></span> &amp;&nbsp;<i>closest </i>to the liquid's <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>surface</u></span> have the highest chances of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>vaporizing</b></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>This is the basis of <b><u>vaporization</u></b>.</div><div><br></div><div>Red line is the <u>average</u> temperature for the whole solution, &amp; the yellow line represents the KE needed for molecules to escape.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""ef840a781ebdfab73576e919f6bd6e0b.png""><br></div></div>"
"Water molecules with the <i>greatest </i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>kinetic energy</u></span> &amp;&nbsp;<i>closest </i>to the liquid's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>surface</u></span> have the highest chances of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;vaporizing&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"Water molecules with the <i>greatest </i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>kinetic energy</u></span> &amp;&nbsp;<i>closest </i>to the liquid's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>surface</u></span> have the highest chances of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>vaporizing</b></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>This is the basis of <b><u>vaporization</u></b>.</div><div><br></div><div>Red line is the <u>average</u> temperature for the whole solution, &amp; the yellow line represents the KE needed for molecules to escape.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""ef840a781ebdfab73576e919f6bd6e0b.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Vapor&#x20;pressure&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is the pressure exerted <i>by</i> the <u>vapor</u>&nbsp;(above the liquid) <i>on</i> the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>liquid's surface</u></span> as a result of vaporization."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Vapor pressure</b></span> is the pressure exerted <i>by</i> the <u>vapor</u>&nbsp;(above the liquid) <i>on</i> the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>liquid's surface</u></span> as a result of vaporization.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""vp equilibrium.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Vapor pressure</b></span> is the pressure exerted <i>by</i> the <u>vapor</u>&nbsp;(above the liquid) <i>on</i> the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;liquid&#x27;s&#x20;surface&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> as a result of vaporization."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Vapor pressure</b></span> is the pressure exerted <i>by</i> the <u>vapor</u>&nbsp;(above the liquid) <i>on</i> the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>liquid's surface</u></span> as a result of vaporization.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""vp equilibrium.jpg""></div>"
"When <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;vapor&#x20;pressure&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> = <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;atmosphperic&#x20;pressure&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>, the solution reaches its <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>boiling point</b></span>."	"When <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>vapor pressure</u></span> = <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>atmosphperic pressure</u></span>, the solution reaches its <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>boiling point</b></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Vapor pressure ""pushes away"" the molecules in the atmospheric pressure, creating a partial vacuum near the surface of the liquid. This makes it easier for the liquid to continue evaporating off.<div><br></div><div><img src=""5b0185794abdac9f91d552fad9bdf024.png""><br></div></div>"
"When <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>vapor pressure</u></span> = <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>atmosphperic pressure</u></span>, the solution reaches its <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;boiling&#x20;point&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"When <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>vapor pressure</u></span> = <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>atmosphperic pressure</u></span>, the solution reaches its <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>boiling point</b></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Vapor pressure ""pushes away"" the molecules in the atmospheric pressure, creating a partial vacuum near the surface of the liquid. This makes it easier for the liquid to continue evaporating off.<div><br></div><div><img src=""5b0185794abdac9f91d552fad9bdf024.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div>At a solution's&nbsp;<b>boiling point</b>, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>air vacuum</u></span> near the solution's surface<i> </i>makes it <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">easier</i></span> for molecules to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""evaporate&#x20;out"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.<br></div>"	"<div>At a solution's&nbsp;<b>boiling point</b>, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>air vacuum</u></span> near the solution's surface<i> </i>makes it <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">easier</i></span> for molecules to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">evaporate out</span>.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<b>Vapor pressure</b> ""pushes away"" the molecules in the atmospheric pressure, creating a partial vacuum near the surface of the liquid. This makes it easier for the liquid to continue evaporating off.<div><br></div><div><img src=""5b0185794abdac9f91d552fad9bdf024.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div>At a solution's&nbsp;<b>boiling point</b>, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>air vacuum</u></span> near the solution's surface<i> </i>makes it <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;easier&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[easier/harder]</span> for molecules to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">evaporate out</span>.<br></div>"	"<div>At a solution's&nbsp;<b>boiling point</b>, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>air vacuum</u></span> near the solution's surface<i> </i>makes it <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">easier</i></span> for molecules to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">evaporate out</span>.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<b>Vapor pressure</b> ""pushes away"" the molecules in the atmospheric pressure, creating a partial vacuum near the surface of the liquid. This makes it easier for the liquid to continue evaporating off.<div><br></div><div><img src=""5b0185794abdac9f91d552fad9bdf024.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div>At a solution's&nbsp;<b>boiling point</b>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;air&#x20;vacuum&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span> near the solution's surface<i> </i>makes it <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">easier</i></span> for molecules to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">evaporate out</span>.<br></div>"	"<div>At a solution's&nbsp;<b>boiling point</b>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>air vacuum</u></span> near the solution's surface<i> </i>makes it <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">easier</i></span> for molecules to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">evaporate out</span>.<br></div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<script>
// Prevent original image from loading before mask
aFade = 50, qFade = 0;
var mask = document.querySelector('#io-overlay>img');
function loaded() {
    var original = document.querySelector('#io-original');
    original.style.visibility = ""visible"";
}
if (mask === null || mask.complete) {
    loaded();
} else {
    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}
</script>

// Toggle answer mask on clicking the image
var toggle = function() {
  var amask = document.getElementById('io-overlay');
  if (amask.style.display === 'block' || amask.style.display === '')
    amask.style.display = 'none';
  else
    amask.style.display = 'block'
}

<div id=""io-wrapper"">
  <div id=""io-overlay""><img src=""5a724ae5e4e543f3b739dddabbf7522f-ao-3-A.svg"" /></div>
  <div id=""io-original""><img src=""tmpg_pdi8_j.png"" /></div>
</div>

<button id=""io-revl-btn"" onclick=""toggle();"">Toggle Masks</button>
<div id=""io-extra-wrapper"">
  <div id=""io-extra"">
    
    
    
    
  </div>
</div>

<script>
// Toggle answer mask on clicking the image
var toggle = function() {
  var amask = document.getElementById('io-overlay');
  if (amask.style.display === 'block' || amask.style.display === '')
    amask.style.display = 'none';
  else
    amask.style.display = 'block'
}

// Prevent original image from loading before mask
aFade = 50, qFade = 0;
var mask = document.querySelector('#io-overlay>img');
function loaded() {
    var original = document.querySelector('#io-original');
    original.style.visibility = ""visible"";
}
if (mask === null || mask.complete) {
    loaded();
} else {
    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}
</script>

"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Optical</b></span> <b>isomers </b>are configurational isomers that differ in their ability to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;rotate&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;plane&#x2D;polarized&#x20;light"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>, like <u>enantiomers</u> or <u>diastereomers</u>.<br></div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Optical</b></span> <b>isomers </b>are configurational isomers that differ in their ability to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>rotate </i>plane-polarized light</span>, like <u>enantiomers</u> or <u>diastereomers</u>.<br></div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Think of <i style=""font-weight: bold;"">absolute configuration</i>.</div><div><b><i><br></i></b></div><div><img src=""216d28f6bd653d32abd2b39bf50ba6dd.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""fa173439c0b165abd62a7936bc2b0ca8.png""><br></div><div></div></div>"
"<div>In order for an atom to be considered <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>chiral</b></span>, it must have <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""4"" data-ordinal=""1"">[#]</span> unique bonds &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""sp&lt;sup&gt;3&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>-hybridization.<br></div>"	"<div>In order for an atom to be considered <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>chiral</b></span>, it must have <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">4</span> unique bonds &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">sp<sup>3</sup></span>-hybridization.<br></div>
